business.summary: Without a formal IT project governance framework, major technology initiatives risk budget overruns, scope creep, and misalignment with business objectives, potentially disrupting customer services and regulatory commitments.

requirements.requirement: Define and communicate criteria for classifying technology projects as major, including thresholds for budget, customer impact, regulatory scope, and strategic significance
requirements.rationale: Clear classification ensures that high-impact projects receive proportionate oversight and resource allocation, preventing costly failures from inadequate governance

requirements.requirement: Allocate dedicated resources (budget, personnel, and tools) for IT project governance activities including independent risk assessments and stage-gate reviews
requirements.rationale: Governance activities require ring-fenced resources to avoid being deprioritised when delivery pressures mount

requirements.requirement: Ensure all major technology projects have a documented business case with measurable success criteria approved by senior management before commencement
requirements.rationale: Business case discipline prevents investment in projects that lack clear strategic value or customer benefit

technology.summary: Technology teams must implement project management tooling and workflows that enforce stage-gate approvals, maintain audit trails, and integrate with risk assessment processes.

requirements.requirement: Implement a project management platform (e.g., Jira, Azure DevOps, MS Project) with configured stage-gate workflows that enforce mandatory approvals before phase transitions
requirements.rationale: Automated workflows prevent projects from advancing without completing required governance checkpoints, creating a verifiable audit trail

requirements.requirement: Maintain a centralised project portfolio dashboard that provides real-time visibility into project status, risk ratings, milestone completion, and resource utilisation
requirements.rationale: Centralised visibility enables senior management to identify troubled projects early and intervene before they impact operations

requirements.requirement: Configure automated alerts and escalation triggers when projects breach defined thresholds for schedule variance, budget overrun, or unresolved risk items
requirements.rationale: Proactive alerting ensures governance issues are surfaced to decision-makers in a timely manner rather than discovered during periodic reviews

governance.summary: A documented governance framework must define roles, responsibilities, approval gates, and oversight mechanisms for all technology projects, with enhanced controls for major initiatives.

requirements.requirement: Establish and maintain a board-approved IT Project Governance Framework that defines project classification criteria, roles and responsibilities, stage-gate approval requirements, and escalation procedures
requirements.rationale: A formally approved framework provides the authoritative basis for all project governance activities and demonstrates regulatory compliance to BNM examiners

requirements.requirement: Require independent risk assessments for all projects classified as major, conducted by personnel not directly involved in project delivery, with results presented to the board or senior management for approval
requirements.rationale: Independence in risk assessment prevents conflicts of interest where project teams may downplay risks to secure approval

requirements.requirement: Mandate quarterly reporting to the board or IT steering committee on the technology project portfolio, including status of major projects, governance compliance metrics, and risk exposure summary
requirements.rationale: Regular board-level reporting ensures sustained oversight and accountability, enabling timely strategic decisions on project continuation or termination

requirements.requirement: Review and update the IT Project Governance Framework at least annually or following significant organisational changes, regulatory updates, or lessons learned from project failures
requirements.rationale: Periodic review ensures the framework remains current and incorporates improvements from experience and evolving regulatory expectations

business.summary: Failing to conduct thorough risk assessments for technology projects exposes the institution to unidentified threats to security, system availability, and data integrity, potentially causing service disruptions and customer harm.

requirements.requirement: Ensure every technology project includes a risk assessment that explicitly evaluates potential impacts on customer-facing services, business operations, and regulatory obligations before project approval
requirements.rationale: Business impact awareness at the project planning stage prevents costly remediation and service disruptions after deployment

requirements.requirement: Require project risk assessments to include interdependency analysis identifying all upstream and downstream systems, shared infrastructure, and third-party dependencies affected by the project
requirements.rationale: Unidentified interdependencies are a leading cause of project-related incidents in Malaysian financial institutions where legacy systems are tightly coupled

requirements.requirement: Establish risk appetite thresholds for technology projects that trigger escalation to senior management or the board when residual risk exceeds acceptable levels
requirements.rationale: Clear escalation thresholds ensure that projects carrying excessive risk receive appropriate management attention and decision-making

technology.summary: Technical teams must implement structured risk assessment processes that systematically identify and evaluate risks to security, availability, and data integrity across all project phases.

requirements.requirement: Implement a standardised project risk assessment template that covers security risks, availability impacts, data integrity threats, system interdependencies, and compliance requirements
requirements.rationale: Standardised templates ensure consistent and comprehensive risk identification across all projects regardless of project manager experience

requirements.requirement: Conduct technical interdependency mapping for each project using architecture diagrams, system integration maps, and CMDB data to identify all affected systems and interfaces
requirements.rationale: Systematic technical mapping prevents overlooking critical dependencies that could cause cascading failures during or after implementation

requirements.requirement: Integrate project risk assessment findings into project plans with specific risk mitigation tasks, assigned owners, target completion dates, and verification criteria
requirements.rationale: Risk mitigations must be actionable project tasks to ensure they are implemented rather than remaining as documented intentions

governance.summary: A formal risk assessment methodology must be established for all technology projects, with documented outputs reviewed and approved by appropriate authorities before project commencement.

requirements.requirement: Establish and maintain a formal project risk assessment methodology that defines the assessment process, risk categories, scoring criteria, and documentation requirements applicable to all technology projects
requirements.rationale: A documented methodology ensures risk assessments are conducted consistently and meet the standard expected by BNM examiners

requirements.requirement: Require risk assessment outputs to be formally reviewed and approved by the project steering committee or designated risk authority before project commencement, with risk acceptance decisions documented for residual risks
requirements.rationale: Formal approval of risk assessments establishes accountability and ensures informed decision-making on project risk exposure

requirements.requirement: Mandate periodic reassessment of project risks at each stage-gate and whenever material changes to project scope, timeline, or technology occur
requirements.rationale: Project risks evolve throughout the lifecycle; static assessments become stale and fail to capture emerging risks from scope changes or technical decisions

business.summary: Post-implementation reviews ensure the institution captures lessons from technology projects and verifies that intended business benefits are being realised, preventing repeated costly mistakes across future initiatives.

requirements.requirement: Conduct a formal post-implementation review within three months of go-live for all significant technology projects, assessing whether stated business objectives and success criteria have been achieved
requirements.rationale: Timely review while knowledge is fresh ensures accurate assessment of outcomes and prevents benefit realisation gaps from going undetected

requirements.requirement: Include business stakeholder feedback in post-implementation reviews, covering operational impact, user adoption, and customer experience outcomes
requirements.rationale: Technical delivery success does not automatically equate to business value; business stakeholder input validates whether the project achieved its intended purpose

requirements.requirement: Track and report on post-implementation review completion rates across the project portfolio to ensure reviews are consistently conducted
requirements.rationale: Without completion tracking, post-implementation reviews are frequently deprioritised as teams move on to new projects

technology.summary: Technical teams must assess whether implemented systems meet performance, security, and reliability targets established during project planning, and identify any operational issues requiring remediation.

requirements.requirement: Evaluate system performance metrics, security control effectiveness, and operational stability during the post-implementation review, comparing actual outcomes against design targets
requirements.rationale: Technical validation confirms that the system operates as intended in production conditions and identifies any gaps requiring corrective action

requirements.requirement: Assess the effectiveness of risk mitigation measures implemented during the project by reviewing incident data, vulnerability assessments, and control test results from the post-go-live period
requirements.rationale: Risk mitigations designed during planning may not be fully effective in practice; early validation prevents prolonged exposure to inadequately controlled risks

requirements.requirement: Document all technical lessons learned including infrastructure challenges, integration issues, and deployment problems encountered, and feed these into SDLC process improvements
requirements.rationale: Systematic capture and application of technical lessons prevents recurring issues and continuously improves delivery capability

governance.summary: Post-implementation reviews must be formally governed with standardised templates, required participants, and a mechanism to ensure lessons learned are incorporated into future projects.

requirements.requirement: Establish a standardised post-implementation review procedure that defines review timelines (within three months of go-live), required participants, assessment criteria, and documentation requirements
requirements.rationale: A standardised procedure ensures consistent quality of reviews across the portfolio and sets clear expectations for all stakeholders

requirements.requirement: Maintain a centralised lessons learned register that captures findings from all post-implementation reviews, with assigned actions for incorporating improvements into governance frameworks and SDLC processes
requirements.rationale: A centralised register transforms individual project learnings into organisational knowledge that improves future project outcomes

requirements.requirement: Report post-implementation review outcomes and key lessons learned to the IT steering committee or senior management on a quarterly basis
requirements.rationale: Management visibility ensures that systemic issues identified across multiple reviews are addressed at the appropriate organisational level

business.summary: A formal SDLC framework with integrated security checkpoints protects the institution from deploying vulnerable or defective systems that could compromise customer data, disrupt services, or trigger regulatory action.

requirements.requirement: Ensure the SDLC framework mandates business owner involvement at requirements, UAT, and deployment phases to validate that security and functional requirements meet business needs
requirements.rationale: Business engagement at critical phases prevents misalignment between technical delivery and business expectations, reducing costly rework and customer impact

requirements.requirement: Allocate budget for security activities at each SDLC phase including threat modelling, security testing, and code review tooling as standard project costs
requirements.rationale: When security activities are not budgeted, they are the first to be cut under delivery pressure, resulting in vulnerable deployments

requirements.requirement: Define and communicate clear escalation paths when quality gates cannot be satisfied, including the authority levels required to grant exceptions
requirements.rationale: Clear escalation paths prevent projects from silently bypassing quality gates while ensuring legitimate exceptions receive appropriate risk-based decisions

technology.summary: The SDLC framework must define security checkpoints and quality gates at each development phase, with specific technical activities, entry and exit criteria, and mandatory tooling requirements.

requirements.requirement: Define mandatory security activities for each SDLC phase: threat modelling at design, secure coding standards at development, SAST/DAST at testing, security configuration review at deployment, and vulnerability monitoring at maintenance
requirements.rationale: Phase-specific security activities ensure threats are addressed at the earliest and most cost-effective point in the development lifecycle

requirements.requirement: Establish measurable quality gate criteria for each phase transition including minimum code coverage, maximum critical/high vulnerability count, performance benchmarks, and security review completion
requirements.rationale: Measurable criteria remove subjectivity from gate decisions and provide clear evidence of compliance for audit purposes

requirements.requirement: Integrate security checkpoints into CI/CD pipelines as automated gates that block progression when security criteria are not met
requirements.rationale: Automation enforces consistent application of security standards without relying on manual discipline, which degrades under time pressure

governance.summary: The SDLC framework must be formally documented, approved by senior management, and subject to periodic review to ensure it remains current and is consistently applied across all development activities.

requirements.requirement: Document and obtain senior management approval for a formal SDLC framework covering all phases from requirements through maintenance, with security integrated at every phase and defined quality gates between phases
requirements.rationale: A formally approved framework establishes the authoritative standard for all development activities and demonstrates regulatory compliance to BNM

requirements.requirement: Conduct periodic compliance assessments to verify that active development projects are adhering to the SDLC framework, with findings reported to management
requirements.rationale: Framework adherence must be actively monitored because development teams under delivery pressure may take shortcuts that undermine security controls

requirements.requirement: Review and update the SDLC framework at least annually to incorporate lessons learned, new security threats, evolving technology standards, and changes to regulatory requirements
requirements.rationale: An outdated framework fails to address current threats and technologies, creating compliance gaps and security vulnerabilities

business.summary: Explicit documentation and traceability of security and compliance requirements ensures that regulatory obligations and risk controls are not lost during development, protecting the institution from deploying non-compliant systems.

requirements.requirement: Mandate that business owners collaborate with information security and compliance teams to define security and compliance requirements during the requirements gathering phase of every development project
requirements.rationale: Early engagement ensures that regulatory obligations and risk controls are captured as formal requirements rather than being retrofitted after development, which is significantly more costly

requirements.requirement: Ensure all security and compliance requirements are linked to their regulatory or policy source (e.g., specific RMiT clause, PDPA section, internal policy reference) for audit traceability
requirements.rationale: Source traceability enables the institution to demonstrate to BNM that each regulatory requirement has been translated into specific system controls

requirements.requirement: Require formal sign-off from information security and compliance functions confirming that all applicable security and compliance requirements have been captured before proceeding to design
requirements.rationale: Independent validation prevents requirements gaps that could result in systems being deployed without adequate security controls or regulatory compliance

technology.summary: Development teams must implement requirements traceability mechanisms that track each security and compliance requirement through design, implementation, testing, and validation to ensure none are dropped during development.

requirements.requirement: Implement and maintain a requirements traceability matrix (RTM) for each project that maps every security and compliance requirement to its design element, code implementation, test case, and validation result
requirements.rationale: End-to-end traceability provides objective evidence that every security requirement was implemented and verified, which is essential for BNM audit readiness

requirements.requirement: Configure project management and testing tools to enforce traceability links between requirements, user stories, test cases, and defects, preventing items without traceability from being marked complete
requirements.rationale: Tool-enforced traceability prevents requirements from being silently dropped during implementation or testing phases

requirements.requirement: Validate all security and compliance requirements through specific test cases prior to deployment, with validation results recorded in the traceability matrix
requirements.rationale: Requirements that are documented but not validated may not function as intended in production, creating a false sense of compliance

governance.summary: A formal requirements management procedure must govern how security and compliance requirements are captured, traced, validated, and approved throughout the development lifecycle.

requirements.requirement: Establish a requirements management procedure that mandates the explicit capture of security and compliance requirements for every development project, with defined roles for requirements definition, review, and approval
requirements.rationale: A formal procedure ensures consistent requirements practices across all projects and prevents security requirements from being treated as optional

requirements.requirement: Require traceability matrix completion and sign-off as a mandatory quality gate before production deployment, with incomplete traceability blocking release approval
requirements.rationale: Making traceability a deployment gate ensures that requirements validation cannot be deferred or skipped under delivery pressure

requirements.requirement: Conduct periodic audits of requirements management practices across active projects to verify compliance with the established procedure and identify improvement areas
requirements.rationale: Ongoing compliance verification ensures that requirements management practices do not degrade over time, particularly in teams under sustained delivery pressure

business.summary: Adopting and enforcing secure coding standards reduces the institution's exposure to application-level vulnerabilities that could be exploited to compromise customer data, disrupt banking services, or cause financial losses.

requirements.requirement: Allocate recurring budget for secure coding training programmes, automated scanning tool licences, and periodic standards refresh to ensure secure development capabilities remain current
requirements.rationale: Secure development requires sustained investment in people and tools; one-off training without ongoing reinforcement leads to skill degradation and non-compliance

requirements.requirement: Require all developers, including contractors and outsourced development partners, to demonstrate competency in the institution's secure coding standards before being granted access to development environments
requirements.rationale: External developers who are not trained on institutional standards introduce inconsistent code quality and potential security vulnerabilities

requirements.requirement: Include secure coding compliance metrics in development team performance reporting to reinforce accountability for code quality
requirements.rationale: Embedding security into performance metrics drives cultural change and ensures secure coding is treated as a core competency, not an afterthought

technology.summary: Development teams must adopt secure coding standards based on OWASP and industry best practices, enforce compliance through code reviews and automated scanning, and ensure all developers are adequately trained.

requirements.requirement: Adopt and maintain secure coding standards based on OWASP guidelines (OWASP Top 10, OWASP ASVS) tailored to each programming language and framework in use, covering input validation, output encoding, authentication, session management, cryptography, and error handling
requirements.rationale: Language-specific standards provide practical, actionable guidance that developers can apply directly, unlike generic security principles

requirements.requirement: Deploy and configure automated code scanning tools (SAST, SCA) integrated into the CI/CD pipeline to detect violations of secure coding standards on every code commit or pull request
requirements.rationale: Automated scanning provides continuous, consistent enforcement of coding standards at scale, catching violations before they reach code review

requirements.requirement: Conduct mandatory peer code reviews for all code changes with reviewers specifically checking against the secure coding standards checklist before approval
requirements.rationale: Manual code review catches logical security flaws and business logic vulnerabilities that automated tools cannot detect, providing defence in depth

governance.summary: Secure coding standards must be formally documented, training must be tracked and refreshed, and compliance must be monitored through reporting and periodic assessments.

requirements.requirement: Publish and maintain formally approved secure coding standards documents for each programming language in use, with annual review and update cycle aligned to OWASP guideline releases
requirements.rationale: Formally published standards provide the authoritative reference for code reviews and automated scanning rule configurations

requirements.requirement: Mandate annual secure coding training for all developers with completion tracking, and require refresher training when standards are materially updated or new vulnerability classes emerge
requirements.rationale: Regular training ensures developers maintain awareness of evolving threats and updated standards, which is a specific BNM requirement for this clause

requirements.requirement: Produce quarterly reports on secure coding compliance metrics including training completion rates, automated scan finding trends, and code review coverage to senior management
requirements.rationale: Management reporting creates accountability and enables trend analysis to identify systemic issues requiring intervention

business.summary: Mandatory code review and static analysis before production deployment prevents security vulnerabilities from reaching customer-facing systems, reducing the risk of data breaches, service outages, and reputational damage.

requirements.requirement: Establish a zero-tolerance policy for deploying code with unresolved critical or high-severity SAST findings to production, with any exceptions requiring formal risk acceptance by the CISO or CTO
requirements.rationale: Deploying known critical vulnerabilities to production creates unacceptable risk to customer data and services; formal exception processes ensure informed decision-making

requirements.requirement: Ensure code review and SAST processes apply equally to all code deployed to production including third-party customisations, integrations, and internally developed scripts
requirements.rationale: Exempting any code category from review creates blind spots that attackers can exploit; third-party code and scripts are common attack vectors

requirements.requirement: Track and report remediation timelines for SAST findings to ensure critical issues are resolved within defined SLAs and do not accumulate as technical debt
requirements.rationale: Without remediation tracking, identified vulnerabilities persist indefinitely, negating the value of the scanning investment

technology.summary: Development teams must implement mandatory peer code reviews and SAST scanning as automated pipeline gates, with defined processes for finding remediation and risk acceptance for exceptions.

requirements.requirement: Integrate SAST tools into the CI/CD pipeline as a mandatory gate that blocks deployment when critical or high-severity findings are detected, with gate bypass requiring documented approval
requirements.rationale: Pipeline-enforced gates prevent human error or time pressure from allowing vulnerable code into production

requirements.requirement: Require a minimum of one independent peer code review for all code changes before merge to the main branch, with reviewers from the approved reviewer list who have completed secure coding training
requirements.rationale: Independent peer review provides human-level security assessment that complements automated scanning, catching business logic flaws and design-level vulnerabilities

requirements.requirement: Configure SAST tools with rule sets aligned to the institution's secure coding standards and OWASP guidelines, with regular tuning to minimise false positives while maintaining detection accuracy
requirements.rationale: Poorly configured SAST tools produce excessive false positives that cause developer fatigue and lead to findings being ignored, undermining the control

requirements.requirement: Maintain a vulnerability findings register that tracks all SAST findings from identification through remediation or risk acceptance, with defined SLAs based on severity
requirements.rationale: A centrally tracked register ensures no finding falls through the cracks and provides the audit trail needed for regulatory examination

governance.summary: Formal policies and procedures must govern code review and SAST processes, including mandatory requirements, exception handling, and management reporting on compliance and findings.

requirements.requirement: Establish a formal code review and static analysis policy that mandates peer review and SAST scanning for all code before production deployment, defines severity classification criteria, and specifies remediation and exception procedures
requirements.rationale: A formal policy provides the authoritative basis for enforcement and demonstrates to BNM that the institution has mandatory controls in place

requirements.requirement: Define a formal risk acceptance procedure for SAST exceptions that requires documentation of the vulnerability, compensating controls, residual risk assessment, and approval by CISO or equivalent authority
requirements.rationale: Exceptions without formal risk acceptance create uncontrolled exposure; a defined procedure ensures exceptions are deliberate, documented, and time-limited

requirements.requirement: Report monthly on code review coverage, SAST compliance rates, open findings by severity, and risk acceptance exceptions to the IT steering committee or senior management
requirements.rationale: Regular management reporting ensures visibility into the effectiveness of code review and SAST controls and enables timely intervention when metrics deteriorate

business.summary: Comprehensive testing standards ensure that systems deployed to production meet functional, performance, and security expectations, preventing customer-impacting defects and service degradation.

requirements.requirement: Mandate that no system or change is deployed to production without documented evidence of functional, integration, performance, and security testing that meets defined acceptance criteria
requirements.rationale: Untested or inadequately tested deployments are the primary cause of production incidents in financial institutions, directly impacting customer experience and regulatory standing

requirements.requirement: Define performance testing criteria based on expected production workloads and peak demand scenarios relevant to the Malaysian banking context (e.g., salary day, festive season peaks, year-end processing)
requirements.rationale: Performance criteria aligned to actual business demand patterns prevent system failures during critical high-volume periods that impact customer trust

requirements.requirement: Require formal approval of test results by the QA lead and business owner before deployment proceeds, with rejected results triggering mandatory defect remediation
requirements.rationale: Dual sign-off ensures both technical quality and business readiness are confirmed, preventing premature deployments driven by schedule pressure

technology.summary: Testing infrastructure and processes must support comprehensive functional, integration, performance, and security testing with documented test plans, automated execution where feasible, and formal result recording.

requirements.requirement: Establish and maintain test environments that accurately replicate production configurations for integration, performance, and security testing, with documented environment specifications and refresh schedules
requirements.rationale: Testing in environments that do not match production leads to false confidence in test results and failures when changes reach the real environment

requirements.requirement: Develop comprehensive test plans for each release covering functional test cases, integration test scenarios, performance test scripts with load profiles, and security test cases including vulnerability scanning and penetration testing
requirements.rationale: Documented test plans ensure systematic coverage and repeatability, preventing ad-hoc testing that misses critical scenarios

requirements.requirement: Implement automated test execution for regression, integration, and performance tests within the CI/CD pipeline, with results automatically recorded and reported
requirements.rationale: Automation ensures consistent test execution and rapid feedback, enabling frequent releases without compromising test thoroughness

requirements.requirement: Conduct security testing including dynamic application security testing (DAST) and vulnerability assessment for every major release, with findings classified by severity and remediated before deployment
requirements.rationale: Security testing validates that the application is resilient to attack in a running state, complementing static analysis conducted during development

governance.summary: Testing standards must be formally documented, test results must be formally reviewed and approved, and testing compliance must be reported as a deployment gate.

requirements.requirement: Document and approve comprehensive testing standards that define mandatory test types, test plan requirements, acceptance criteria, result documentation requirements, and approval procedures for all production deployments
requirements.rationale: Formally approved standards provide the authoritative basis for consistent testing practices and regulatory compliance demonstration

requirements.requirement: Mandate formal review and approval of test results by both the QA lead and a designated business representative as a prerequisite for production deployment approval
requirements.rationale: Formal review ensures test results are scrutinised for adequacy and completeness rather than accepted without examination

requirements.requirement: Maintain testing metrics including test coverage, defect density, test pass rates, and defect leakage to production, reporting trends quarterly to management
requirements.rationale: Testing metrics provide objective evidence of testing effectiveness and highlight areas requiring improvement in testing practices

business.summary: Formal user acceptance testing by business users in a production-like environment ensures that deployed systems meet actual business needs and do not introduce risks to customer data through exposure of unmasked production information.

requirements.requirement: Require business owners to formally sign off UAT results confirming that the system meets business requirements, operational workflows, and customer experience expectations before production deployment is authorised
requirements.rationale: Business owner sign-off establishes accountability for confirming that the system is fit for purpose from a business perspective, not just technically functional

requirements.requirement: Allocate adequate business user time and resources for UAT activities, ensuring that testing is conducted by personnel who understand the business processes the system supports
requirements.rationale: UAT conducted by IT staff or under-resourced business teams fails to validate real-world usage scenarios and may miss critical business logic issues

requirements.requirement: Define UAT acceptance criteria in collaboration with business stakeholders during the requirements phase, not at the point of UAT execution
requirements.rationale: Pre-defined acceptance criteria prevent scope creep during UAT and ensure testing is focused on validating the agreed business requirements

technology.summary: UAT environments must mirror production infrastructure configurations and data masking must be applied to any production data used in non-production environments to protect customer information.

requirements.requirement: Provision and maintain a UAT environment that mirrors production in terms of infrastructure configuration, software versions, integration points, and network architecture, with documented comparison records
requirements.rationale: Environment parity ensures that UAT results are reliable predictors of production behaviour; configuration differences between UAT and production are a leading cause of post-deployment failures

requirements.requirement: Implement automated data masking or synthetic data generation for all UAT environments, ensuring that production customer data is anonymised or replaced before use in non-production environments
requirements.rationale: Using unmasked production data in UAT environments violates PDPA requirements and creates data breach risk through less-secured non-production environments

requirements.requirement: Implement UAT defect tracking with severity classification and mandatory remediation of critical and high-severity defects before business sign-off is permitted
requirements.rationale: Structured defect management during UAT ensures that significant issues are resolved before deployment rather than accepted as known issues

governance.summary: UAT procedures must be formally documented with defined sign-off requirements, environment standards, and data protection controls to ensure consistent and compliant acceptance testing.

requirements.requirement: Establish formal UAT procedures defining environment requirements, data masking standards, test execution processes, defect management, acceptance criteria, and business owner sign-off requirements
requirements.rationale: Documented procedures ensure UAT is conducted consistently across all projects and provides the framework for demonstrating compliance to BNM

requirements.requirement: Mandate documented business owner sign-off as a prerequisite for production deployment, with the sign-off explicitly confirming UAT completion, acceptance criteria met, and outstanding defect disposition agreed
requirements.rationale: Formal sign-off creates an auditable record of business acceptance and ensures deployment decisions are not made unilaterally by IT

requirements.requirement: Conduct periodic audits of UAT environments to verify production data masking compliance and environment-production parity, reporting findings to management
requirements.rationale: Without regular verification, data masking controls may degrade over time or be bypassed for convenience, creating regulatory and privacy risks

business.summary: A formal change management policy prevents uncontrolled modifications to production systems that could disrupt banking services, compromise data integrity, or violate regulatory requirements.

requirements.requirement: Ensure all changes to production systems — applications, infrastructure, databases, and configurations — are assessed for potential impact on customer services, business operations, and regulatory compliance before approval
requirements.rationale: Impact assessment prevents changes from being deployed without understanding their potential to disrupt customer-facing services or create compliance violations

requirements.requirement: Establish a Change Advisory Board (CAB) with representation from IT operations, information security, business units, and risk management to review and approve significant changes
requirements.rationale: Cross-functional review ensures that changes are evaluated from multiple perspectives including business impact, security risk, and operational readiness

requirements.requirement: Communicate planned changes and maintenance windows to affected business units and customers with adequate notice, particularly for changes impacting customer-facing channels
requirements.rationale: Stakeholder communication minimises business disruption and maintains customer confidence during planned change activities

technology.summary: Technical change management processes must ensure that all production changes are risk-assessed, tested, documented in a change log, and deployed through controlled procedures with rollback capability.

requirements.requirement: Implement a change management system (e.g., ServiceNow, Jira Service Management) that enforces the full change lifecycle: request, risk assessment, testing evidence, approval workflow, implementation, and post-change verification
requirements.rationale: System-enforced workflows prevent changes from bypassing required steps and create a complete audit trail for regulatory examination

requirements.requirement: Require every change request to include a risk assessment covering potential impact on system availability, security, data integrity, and interconnected systems, with risk rating determining the approval authority level
requirements.rationale: Risk-based approval routing ensures that higher-risk changes receive more rigorous scrutiny while allowing low-risk changes to proceed efficiently

requirements.requirement: Mandate that every change includes a tested rollback plan and that post-change verification steps are defined and executed to confirm the change was implemented successfully
requirements.rationale: Rollback plans enable rapid recovery from failed changes, while post-change verification catches implementation issues before they impact users

governance.summary: A formal change management policy must be established with defined change categories, approval authorities, documentation requirements, and a complete change log for all production modifications.

requirements.requirement: Establish and maintain a board-approved change management policy that governs all changes to production systems including applications, infrastructure, databases, network configurations, and security controls
requirements.rationale: A comprehensive policy ensures no category of production change escapes formal governance, which is a fundamental BNM expectation

requirements.requirement: Define change categories (standard, normal, major, emergency) with corresponding approval authorities, documentation requirements, and implementation procedures for each category
requirements.rationale: Category-based governance enables proportionate controls that balance rigour with operational efficiency

requirements.requirement: Maintain a comprehensive change log that records all production changes including request details, risk assessment, approvals, implementation records, and post-change verification results
requirements.rationale: A complete change log provides the audit trail essential for incident investigation, regulatory examination, and compliance demonstration

requirements.requirement: Review change management metrics quarterly including change success rates, failed change rates, unauthorised change detection, and CAB effectiveness, reporting findings to senior management
requirements.rationale: Metrics-based review enables continuous improvement of the change management process and early detection of control weaknesses

business.summary: Defined emergency change procedures enable rapid response to critical production issues while maintaining accountability through retrospective review, preventing both service disruptions from delayed fixes and uncontrolled changes from bypassed governance.

requirements.requirement: Define clear criteria for what constitutes an emergency change, limited to situations where delay would result in significant customer impact, security breach, or regulatory violation
requirements.rationale: Without strict criteria, the emergency change process becomes a shortcut for convenience, undermining the standard change management controls

requirements.requirement: Require retrospective review and formal approval of all emergency changes within a defined timeframe (e.g., five business days), with incomplete retrospective reviews escalated to senior management
requirements.rationale: Retrospective review ensures that emergency changes receive the same governance scrutiny as standard changes, just on a deferred basis

requirements.requirement: Monitor and report emergency change frequency to senior management monthly, investigating trends that may indicate systemic issues with standard change processes or system stability
requirements.rationale: High emergency change volumes may indicate process failures, inadequate testing, or systemic stability issues requiring management intervention

technology.summary: Emergency change procedures must enable rapid implementation while maintaining minimum technical controls including change logging, access restrictions, and post-implementation verification.

requirements.requirement: Implement a streamlined emergency change workflow in the change management system that captures the justification, approving authority, implementer, and change details while enabling rapid execution
requirements.rationale: A streamlined but structured workflow balances speed of response with the minimum documentation needed for accountability and audit

requirements.requirement: Restrict emergency change implementation access to a defined set of authorised personnel with appropriate privileged access, logged through privileged access management tools
requirements.rationale: Limiting who can implement emergency changes reduces the risk of unauthorised modifications under the guise of emergency procedures

requirements.requirement: Require post-implementation verification for all emergency changes including system health checks, service availability confirmation, and monitoring alert review
requirements.rationale: Emergency changes carry higher risk due to reduced pre-implementation testing; immediate post-change verification ensures they have not introduced new issues

governance.summary: Emergency change procedures must be formally documented with strict eligibility criteria, defined approval authorities, mandatory retrospective review requirements, and management reporting.

requirements.requirement: Document formal emergency change procedures that define eligibility criteria, approval authority (minimum two authorised personnel), implementation requirements, retrospective review process, and reporting obligations
requirements.rationale: Documented procedures ensure emergency changes are handled consistently and that governance is maintained even under time pressure

requirements.requirement: Mandate that all emergency changes are presented to the CAB for retrospective review within five business days, with the review assessing whether the emergency classification was appropriate and the change was implemented safely
requirements.rationale: CAB retrospective review provides independent oversight and deters misuse of emergency procedures as a governance bypass mechanism

requirements.requirement: Produce monthly management reports on emergency change volumes, classification appropriateness, retrospective review completion rates, and trend analysis
requirements.rationale: Management reporting creates accountability and enables identification of patterns that indicate systemic issues requiring process improvement

business.summary: A structured release management process ensures controlled and orderly deployment of changes to production, minimising the risk of service disruptions and enabling rapid recovery through tested rollback procedures.

requirements.requirement: Require every production release to have a documented release plan including rollback procedures, deployment verification steps, and defined success criteria agreed with business stakeholders
requirements.rationale: Pre-defined release plans prevent ad-hoc deployments and ensure that recovery options are available if a release causes unexpected issues

requirements.requirement: Define and enforce release windows that minimise customer impact, scheduling significant releases during low-traffic periods and avoiding critical business dates (month-end, salary processing days, festive periods)
requirements.rationale: Timing releases to minimise customer exposure reduces the business impact of any deployment issues and demonstrates customer-centric risk management

requirements.requirement: Communicate release schedules to affected business units and support teams in advance, with post-release notifications confirming successful deployment or triggering incident response
requirements.rationale: Proactive communication enables business continuity planning and ensures support teams are prepared to handle any post-release issues

technology.summary: Release management must implement controlled deployment procedures with mandatory rollback capability, deployment verification automation, and defined success criteria validated before release completion.

requirements.requirement: Implement automated deployment pipelines with built-in rollback capability that can revert to the previous stable version within a defined timeframe (e.g., 30 minutes) if deployment verification fails
requirements.rationale: Automated rollback reduces recovery time from failed deployments and eliminates human error during high-pressure rollback scenarios

requirements.requirement: Define and automate post-deployment verification checks including application health checks, key transaction validations, integration endpoint tests, and monitoring alert baseline confirmation
requirements.rationale: Automated verification provides rapid, consistent confirmation that the release is functioning correctly before the deployment is considered complete

requirements.requirement: Establish defined success criteria for each release that must be confirmed within a specified timeframe post-deployment, including system availability targets, error rate thresholds, and performance benchmarks
requirements.rationale: Objective success criteria remove ambiguity about whether a release should be considered successful or whether rollback should be initiated

requirements.requirement: Maintain version control of all production release packages with the ability to identify and reproduce the exact state of any historical release
requirements.rationale: Version-controlled release packages ensure that rollback to any previous version is reliable and that audit investigations can determine exactly what was deployed

governance.summary: Release management procedures must be formally documented with mandatory release plan requirements, approval gates, and post-deployment verification records.

requirements.requirement: Establish a formal release management procedure that defines release planning requirements, mandatory release plan contents (rollback, verification, success criteria), approval gates, and documentation standards
requirements.rationale: A formal procedure ensures consistent release practices across all deployment activities and provides the governance framework for regulatory compliance

requirements.requirement: Require release plan approval from Change Manager and relevant business stakeholders before deployment execution, with the approval confirming that rollback procedures and success criteria are defined
requirements.rationale: Pre-deployment approval ensures all required planning artefacts are in place and stakeholders are prepared before the release proceeds

requirements.requirement: Track and report release management metrics including release success rate, rollback frequency, deployment verification compliance, and mean time to recover from failed releases
requirements.rationale: Metrics-based reporting enables continuous improvement of release processes and provides early warning of degrading release quality

business.summary: Strict separation between development, testing, staging, and production environments prevents untested code and unauthorised access from compromising production systems that handle customer data and banking transactions.

requirements.requirement: Ensure that environment separation controls protect customer data in production from exposure to development and testing personnel who do not require access to live data
requirements.rationale: Unrestricted access between environments creates data breach risk and violates the principle of least privilege, potentially breaching PDPA requirements

requirements.requirement: Require that all code migrations between environments follow a defined promotion path (development to testing to staging to production) with no direct deployments from development to production
requirements.rationale: A structured promotion path ensures code passes through all required quality and security gates before reaching production

requirements.requirement: Budget for and maintain separate infrastructure for each environment tier, avoiding shared resources that could create cross-environment contamination or performance interference
requirements.rationale: Shared infrastructure between environments creates risk of development activities impacting production stability and security

technology.summary: Technical controls must enforce strict separation between environments through network segmentation, access controls, separate infrastructure, and auditable code migration processes.

requirements.requirement: Implement network segmentation between development, testing, staging, and production environments using firewalls, VLANs, or cloud security groups, preventing direct network communication between non-adjacent environments
requirements.rationale: Network-level separation provides a foundational control that prevents unauthorised data flows and lateral movement between environments

requirements.requirement: Enforce distinct access control lists for each environment, ensuring that developers have access to development and testing environments only, with production access restricted to authorised operations personnel through privileged access management
requirements.rationale: Role-based environment access prevents developers from making ad-hoc changes to production and ensures segregation of duties between development and operations

requirements.requirement: Implement automated CI/CD pipelines as the sole mechanism for migrating code between environments, with pipeline configurations enforcing the defined promotion path and producing audit logs for every migration
requirements.rationale: Pipeline-enforced code migration prevents manual deployments that bypass quality gates and creates a complete audit trail for regulatory examination

requirements.requirement: Deploy separate database instances for each environment with no shared database connections between production and non-production environments
requirements.rationale: Shared database connections are a critical risk vector enabling non-production code to read or modify production data

governance.summary: Environment separation must be governed by formal policy with documented architecture, access controls, and migration procedures subject to regular compliance verification.

requirements.requirement: Establish a formal environment separation policy that mandates strict separation between development, testing, staging, and production environments, defining access control requirements and code migration procedures for each transition
requirements.rationale: A formal policy establishes the authoritative standard for environment controls and provides the basis for compliance monitoring and audit

requirements.requirement: Maintain current architecture documentation showing the separation between environments including network diagrams, infrastructure allocation, access control configurations, and code migration pathways
requirements.rationale: Architecture documentation provides auditable evidence of separation and enables verification that controls match the documented design

requirements.requirement: Conduct quarterly access reviews for production environments to verify that only authorised operations personnel have access and that no developers retain production access beyond approved temporary grants
requirements.rationale: Regular access reviews detect and remediate access creep, which is a common finding where temporary access grants become permanent

business.summary: Source code represents critical intellectual property and a high-value attack vector. Protecting it from unauthorised access, modification, and loss safeguards the institution's proprietary systems, prevents supply-chain attacks, and ensures regulatory traceability of all code changes.

requirements.requirement: Classify source code as confidential or restricted information and apply corresponding data handling controls, including restrictions on code storage in personal devices, cloud storage, and external repositories
requirements.rationale: Treating source code as a classified asset ensures it receives appropriate protection proportionate to its value and the risk of exposure to threat actors

requirements.requirement: Require all source code changes to be linked to approved change requests or user stories, ensuring full traceability from business requirement to deployed code
requirements.rationale: Traceability from business requirements to code changes enables audit verification that only authorised and approved changes are deployed to production

requirements.requirement: Establish intellectual property protection clauses in all developer contracts (including third-party vendors and contractors) covering source code confidentiality, non-disclosure, and return-of-assets obligations
requirements.rationale: Contractual protections provide legal recourse and clear expectations for all personnel with access to the institution's source code assets
requirements.owner: Head of Legal / Head of IT

technology.summary: Source code protection requires version-controlled repositories with role-based access, mandatory code review workflows, branch protection rules, and verification that source code is never deployed or stored in production environments.

requirements.requirement: Store all source code exclusively in approved, centrally managed version control repositories (e.g., GitHub Enterprise, GitLab, Bitbucket) with enforced authentication, encryption at rest and in transit, and automated backup
requirements.rationale: Centralised version control provides a single source of truth, immutable change history, and consistent access controls across all code assets

requirements.requirement: Enforce branch protection rules on main and release branches requiring peer code review approval, passing automated tests, and signed commits before merging is permitted
requirements.rationale: Branch protection prevents direct, unreviewed changes to critical branches and ensures every production-bound change has been validated by at least one independent reviewer

requirements.requirement: Implement automated secret scanning on all repositories to detect and prevent accidental commits of credentials, API keys, certificates, and other sensitive data
requirements.rationale: Accidental exposure of secrets in source code is a leading cause of breaches; automated scanning provides a preventive control before secrets reach shared branches

requirements.requirement: Verify through deployment pipeline controls that production environments contain only compiled artefacts or container images, and that source code files, development tools, and debugging utilities are excluded from production deployments
requirements.rationale: Source code in production exposes application logic to attackers, increases the attack surface, and violates the RMiT requirement to keep source code out of production

governance.summary: Source code protection must be governed by a formal policy covering repository standards, access control reviews, change tracking, and periodic verification that production environments are free of source code.

requirements.requirement: Establish a formal source code management policy defining approved repositories, access control standards, code review requirements, branching strategies, and prohibitions on source code in production environments
requirements.rationale: A written policy sets organisation-wide standards for code protection and provides the governance baseline against which compliance can be measured

requirements.requirement: Conduct quarterly access reviews of all source code repositories, removing access for personnel who have changed roles, left the organisation, or no longer require code access for their current responsibilities
requirements.rationale: Regular access reviews prevent accumulation of unnecessary access rights and ensure that only active, authorised personnel can view or modify source code

requirements.requirement: Perform semi-annual audits of production environments to confirm that source code, development tools, and compiler utilities are not present, documenting findings and remediating any exceptions within 30 days
requirements.rationale: Periodic verification provides assurance that the deployment pipeline controls are effective and that source code has not inadvertently reached production

business.summary: Configuration management ensures the institution maintains an accurate, complete inventory of all technology assets and their configurations, enabling rapid incident response, reliable change impact assessment, and demonstrable control over the technology estate.

requirements.requirement: Maintain a comprehensive configuration management database (CMDB) or equivalent inventory covering all technology assets including servers, databases, network devices, middleware, applications, and their interdependencies
requirements.rationale: An accurate asset inventory is foundational for risk management, incident response, and regulatory reporting; without it, the institution cannot demonstrate control over its technology estate

requirements.requirement: Require change impact assessments to reference the CMDB to identify affected assets and downstream dependencies before approving changes to production systems
requirements.rationale: CMDB-informed impact assessments reduce the risk of unintended consequences from changes to interconnected systems, particularly for core banking and payment platforms

requirements.requirement: Assign a named owner for each configuration item in the CMDB, with accountability for maintaining the accuracy of that item's configuration records and notifying the configuration management team of any changes
requirements.rationale: Clear ownership ensures configuration records remain current and that there is an accountable party for each asset during incidents, audits, and compliance reviews

technology.summary: Configuration management requires automated discovery and monitoring tools, defined configuration baselines for all critical systems, and drift detection mechanisms that identify and alert on deviations from approved configurations.

requirements.requirement: Implement automated configuration discovery and monitoring tools (e.g., Ansible, Puppet, Chef, SCCM, cloud-native config services) to continuously reconcile actual configurations against the CMDB and detect unauthorised changes
requirements.rationale: Automated discovery eliminates reliance on manual inventory updates and provides near-real-time visibility into configuration drift across the technology estate

requirements.requirement: Establish approved configuration baselines for all critical system types (operating systems, databases, middleware, network devices) incorporating security hardening standards (CIS Benchmarks or equivalent) and deploy them via infrastructure-as-code templates
requirements.rationale: Configuration baselines ensure systems are deployed in a known-good, security-hardened state and provide a measurable reference point for drift detection

requirements.requirement: Implement automated configuration drift detection that compares running configurations against approved baselines and generates alerts for deviations, with critical deviations escalated to the security operations team within 4 hours
requirements.rationale: Timely detection of configuration drift is essential for identifying both accidental misconfigurations and malicious changes to system settings

requirements.requirement: Store all infrastructure and application configurations as code (IaC) in version-controlled repositories, ensuring that production configurations can be rebuilt from source and every configuration change is traceable
requirements.rationale: Infrastructure-as-code provides an immutable audit trail of configuration changes, enables rapid rebuild after incidents, and prevents configuration drift from manual changes

governance.summary: Configuration management must be governed by formal procedures defining baseline standards, deviation handling processes, CMDB accuracy requirements, and periodic compliance verification reporting.

requirements.requirement: Establish a formal configuration management procedure defining the scope of the CMDB, configuration item classification, baseline establishment process, change recording requirements, and deviation handling procedures
requirements.rationale: A formal procedure ensures consistent configuration management practices across all technology teams and provides the policy framework for regulatory compliance

requirements.requirement: Produce monthly configuration compliance reports showing the percentage of systems conforming to approved baselines, outstanding deviations, remediation status, and trend analysis over the preceding 12 months
requirements.rationale: Regular compliance reporting provides management visibility into configuration health and demonstrates to BNM examiners that deviations are actively managed

requirements.requirement: Conduct quarterly CMDB accuracy audits by sampling configuration items and comparing recorded attributes against actual system configurations, with a target accuracy rate of at least 95%
requirements.rationale: CMDB accuracy audits ensure the inventory remains reliable for incident response, change management, and regulatory reporting purposes

business.summary: Capacity planning ensures technology resources are sufficient to meet current and projected business requirements, preventing service degradation during peak periods such as salary processing, festive seasons, and regulatory reporting windows.

requirements.requirement: Develop annual capacity plans aligned with business growth projections, incorporating planned product launches, anticipated transaction volume increases, and seasonal demand patterns specific to Malaysian banking (e.g., Hari Raya, Chinese New Year, year-end)
requirements.rationale: Business-aligned capacity planning prevents service outages during high-demand periods and ensures technology investment is proportionate to expected growth

requirements.requirement: Define service-level capacity thresholds for all critical business systems (core banking, internet banking, mobile banking, payment gateways) specifying warning and critical levels that trigger scaling actions
requirements.rationale: Predefined thresholds enable proactive capacity management before service degradation impacts customers, rather than reactive scaling after performance issues occur

requirements.requirement: Require capacity impact assessments for all major business initiatives, new product launches, and regulatory changes (e.g., new BNM reporting requirements) before implementation to ensure supporting infrastructure can handle projected demand
requirements.rationale: Pre-implementation capacity assessments prevent situations where new services are launched on infrastructure that cannot sustain the expected transaction volumes

technology.summary: Capacity planning requires continuous monitoring of resource utilisation, defined thresholds with automated alerting, regular load and stress testing, and scalable architecture designs that enable timely capacity adjustments.

requirements.requirement: Implement continuous capacity monitoring for all critical systems covering CPU, memory, storage, network bandwidth, database connections, and application-specific metrics (e.g., transaction throughput, queue depth), with dashboards accessible to operations teams
requirements.rationale: Comprehensive monitoring provides the data foundation for capacity planning decisions and enables early detection of capacity constraints before they affect service delivery

requirements.requirement: Configure automated alerting when capacity utilisation reaches defined warning thresholds (e.g., 70%) and critical thresholds (e.g., 85%), with escalation procedures that trigger scaling actions or procurement requests
requirements.rationale: Automated threshold alerting ensures capacity constraints are identified and acted upon before reaching levels that cause service degradation

requirements.requirement: Conduct annual load testing and stress testing for all critical systems to validate that current capacity meets projected peak demand with at least 30% headroom, documenting results and remediation actions for systems failing to meet targets
requirements.rationale: Load testing validates theoretical capacity plans against real-world performance and identifies bottlenecks that monitoring alone may not reveal

requirements.requirement: Design and maintain scalable architectures (horizontal scaling, auto-scaling groups, elastic cloud resources) for customer-facing systems that can respond to demand surges within defined timeframes without manual intervention
requirements.rationale: Scalable architectures reduce the lead time for capacity adjustments and prevent customer-facing outages during unexpected demand spikes

governance.summary: Capacity planning governance requires formal procedures, regular reporting to management on utilisation trends and forecasts, and documented evidence of timely scaling actions when thresholds are approached.

requirements.requirement: Establish a formal capacity planning procedure defining monitoring requirements, threshold standards, reporting cadence, escalation procedures, and the process for requesting and approving capacity scaling or procurement
requirements.rationale: A formal procedure ensures consistent capacity management practices and provides the governance framework for timely scaling decisions

requirements.requirement: Present quarterly capacity reports to IT steering committee covering current utilisation against thresholds, 12-month demand forecasts, planned capacity investments, and any systems operating above warning thresholds
requirements.rationale: Regular management reporting ensures capacity risks are visible to decision-makers and that investment approvals for capacity scaling are obtained before constraints become critical

requirements.requirement: Document and retain records of all capacity scaling actions taken, including the triggering event, actions implemented, timeline from alert to resolution, and post-action verification that thresholds returned to normal levels
requirements.rationale: Documented scaling actions demonstrate to BNM examiners that the institution responds appropriately to capacity constraints and maintains adequate technology resources

business.summary: A formal patch management policy ensures the institution systematically identifies, evaluates, tests, and deploys patches across all technology assets, reducing exposure to known vulnerabilities that could be exploited to compromise banking systems and customer data.

requirements.requirement: Establish a board-approved patch management policy that defines the end-to-end patch lifecycle from identification through deployment, covering all technology assets including servers, endpoints, network devices, databases, middleware, and applications
requirements.rationale: A comprehensive, board-approved policy demonstrates senior management commitment to vulnerability management and ensures consistent patching practices across the entire technology estate

requirements.requirement: Define patch classification criteria aligned with industry standards (CVSS scoring) specifying severity levels (Critical, High, Medium, Low) and corresponding maximum deployment timeframes for each level
requirements.rationale: Classification criteria ensure patches are prioritised based on risk severity, directing limited resources to the most impactful vulnerabilities first and meeting BNM's specific timeline expectations

requirements.requirement: Include patch management responsibilities in third-party vendor and managed service provider contracts, requiring vendors to comply with the institution's patch deployment timelines and provide patching status reports
requirements.rationale: Third-party managed systems represent a significant portion of the technology estate; contractual patch management requirements ensure consistent coverage regardless of who operates the systems

technology.summary: Patch management technology requirements cover automated vulnerability scanning, centralised patch distribution, mandatory pre-deployment testing, and comprehensive tracking of patch compliance across all technology assets.

requirements.requirement: Implement automated vulnerability scanning tools that continuously identify missing patches across all technology assets, with scan results feeding into a centralised patch tracking system for prioritisation and deployment scheduling
requirements.rationale: Automated scanning eliminates reliance on manual patch identification and provides continuous visibility into the institution's vulnerability exposure across all asset types

requirements.requirement: Establish a dedicated patch testing environment that mirrors production configurations for validating patches before deployment, with defined test cases covering application functionality, integration points, and performance benchmarks
requirements.rationale: Pre-deployment testing in a representative environment prevents patch-related outages in production, which is particularly critical for core banking and payment systems

requirements.requirement: Deploy centralised patch management tools (e.g., WSUS, SCCM, Ansible, cloud-native patching services) capable of distributing patches to all managed endpoints and servers with reporting on deployment success and failure rates
requirements.rationale: Centralised distribution ensures consistent patch deployment across the technology estate and provides the reporting data needed for compliance monitoring

requirements.requirement: Maintain rollback procedures for all patch deployments, including system snapshots or restore points taken before patching, to enable rapid recovery if a patch causes system instability
requirements.rationale: Rollback capability ensures service continuity if a patch causes unexpected issues, reducing the business impact of necessary security updates

governance.summary: Patch management governance requires formal policy documentation, regular compliance reporting against defined timelines, and escalation procedures for patches that cannot be deployed within required timeframes.

requirements.requirement: Document and maintain a formal patch management policy approved by the CISO and reviewed annually, covering scope, classification criteria, deployment timelines, testing requirements, exception procedures, and reporting requirements
requirements.rationale: A formally documented policy provides the authoritative reference for all patch management activities and satisfies BNM's requirement for a written patch management policy

requirements.requirement: Produce monthly patch compliance reports showing the percentage of assets fully patched against each severity level, overdue patches with ageing analysis, and exception/risk acceptance records for deferred patches
requirements.rationale: Regular compliance reporting provides management and BNM examiners with visibility into patching effectiveness and highlights areas requiring attention

requirements.requirement: Define and enforce an escalation process for patches that cannot be deployed within mandated timelines, requiring compensating controls to be documented and formal risk acceptance obtained from the appropriate authority (CISO for high, CRO for critical)
requirements.rationale: Escalation procedures ensure that overdue patches receive management attention and that residual risk from delayed patching is formally acknowledged and mitigated

business.summary: RMiT mandates specific patch deployment timelines -- critical patches within 14 days, high-severity within 30 days. Where these timelines cannot be met, compensating controls and formal risk acceptance must be documented to manage residual vulnerability exposure.

requirements.requirement: Enforce mandatory deployment of critical security patches within 14 calendar days of vendor release and high-severity patches within 30 calendar days, with tracking mechanisms to monitor compliance against these timelines across all technology assets
requirements.rationale: BNM explicitly requires these timelines; failure to meet them constitutes a direct compliance gap that will be flagged during regulatory examinations

requirements.requirement: Where a critical or high-severity patch cannot be deployed within the mandated timeline, require the responsible team to submit a formal patch exception request documenting the reason for delay, compensating controls implemented, target remediation date, and risk assessment
requirements.rationale: Documented exception management ensures that overdue patches are not simply ignored and that the institution can demonstrate to BNM that residual risk is being actively managed

requirements.requirement: Require formal risk acceptance sign-off from the CISO for high-severity patch exceptions and from the Chief Risk Officer for critical patch exceptions, with the risk acceptance including a defined maximum extension period (not exceeding 90 days)
requirements.rationale: Risk acceptance at the appropriate authority level ensures that senior management is aware of and accountable for vulnerability exposure from delayed patching

technology.summary: Patch deployment timeline compliance requires automated tracking of patch release dates against deployment dates, real-time dashboards showing overdue patches, and documented compensating controls for any exceptions.

requirements.requirement: Implement automated patch tracking that records the vendor release date, institution receipt date, testing completion date, and production deployment date for every patch, calculating elapsed time against the 14-day and 30-day mandates
requirements.rationale: Automated timeline tracking provides objective, auditable evidence of patch deployment compliance and eliminates manual calculation errors in compliance reporting

requirements.requirement: Deploy real-time patch compliance dashboards showing the number and percentage of outstanding critical patches approaching or exceeding the 14-day deadline, with automated escalation alerts at 7-day and 12-day marks
requirements.rationale: Proactive alerting before deadlines expire gives operations teams sufficient time to complete deployment or initiate exception procedures before compliance gaps occur

requirements.requirement: Implement compensating controls for systems with overdue patches, including enhanced monitoring (IDS/IPS rules targeting the specific vulnerability), network segmentation to limit exposure, and application-level controls (WAF rules, access restrictions)
requirements.rationale: Compensating controls reduce the risk exposure from unpatched vulnerabilities while the institution works to complete patch deployment, satisfying BNM's requirement for documented mitigations

requirements.requirement: Prioritise emergency patch deployment procedures for zero-day vulnerabilities being actively exploited, with an accelerated timeline of 72 hours for internet-facing systems and 7 days for internal systems
requirements.rationale: Actively exploited vulnerabilities represent an immediate threat to the institution; accelerated timelines ensure the most dangerous vulnerabilities are addressed with appropriate urgency

governance.summary: Patch timeline governance requires documented standards, formal exception and risk acceptance workflows, regular compliance reporting to management, and audit trail preservation for all patching activities.

requirements.requirement: Document patch deployment timeline standards specifying the 14-day critical and 30-day high-severity mandates, exception procedures, compensating control requirements, and risk acceptance authority levels in the patch management policy
