requirements.requirement: Implement a privileged access management programme that controls the granting, monitoring, and periodic review of all privileged access rights across critical banking systems and infrastructure
requirements.rationale: Privileged accounts have the ability to bypass security controls, access sensitive customer data, and alter system configurations; unmanaged privileged access represents the highest-impact access risk

requirements.requirement: Ensure all privileged activities are logged with sufficient detail to support forensic investigations, and that logs are retained for a minimum of three years in tamper-evident storage
requirements.rationale: Comprehensive logging of privileged activities is essential for detecting misuse, supporting incident investigations, and satisfying BNM's three-year retention requirement

requirements.requirement: Eliminate shared privileged accounts across all systems, with documented exceptions only where operationally justified, formally approved by the CISO, and subject to compensating controls including session recording and individual accountability
requirements.rationale: Shared accounts prevent individual accountability and make it impossible to attribute privileged actions to specific personnel during investigations

technology.summary: Privileged access must be managed through a dedicated PAM solution providing session recording, credential vaulting, just-in-time access, and comprehensive audit logging with tamper-evident retention for a minimum of three years.

requirements.requirement: Deploy a PAM solution (e.g., CyberArk, BeyondTrust, Delinea) providing credential vaulting, automated password rotation, session recording, and just-in-time privileged access for all critical systems
requirements.rationale: A dedicated PAM solution centralises privileged access control, eliminates standing privileges, and provides comprehensive audit trails for all administrative activities

requirements.requirement: Configure privileged activity logging to capture all administrative actions with timestamps, user identity, source IP, target system, and actions performed, with logs forwarded to a tamper-evident SIEM or log management platform retained for at least three years
requirements.rationale: Detailed, tamper-evident logging ensures that all privileged activities can be audited and investigated, meeting BNM's three-year retention requirement

requirements.requirement: Implement technical controls to enforce individual accountability for all privileged sessions, including personal account mapping for any remaining shared accounts and session recording with user attribution
requirements.rationale: Technical enforcement of individual accountability ensures that even where shared accounts exist, each session can be attributed to a specific individual

governance.summary: Privileged access governance requires a formal policy, a maintained register of all privileged accounts and exceptions, periodic access reviews, and management reporting on PAM programme effectiveness.

requirements.requirement: Maintain a formal privileged access management policy defining standards for privileged account management, logging requirements, shared account restrictions, and the exceptions process with required compensating controls
requirements.rationale: A formal policy provides clear governance over privileged access and establishes the institutional position on shared accounts and logging requirements

requirements.requirement: Maintain a register of all privileged accounts including account type, assigned owner, associated systems, and justification, with a separate exception register for any approved shared privileged accounts and their compensating controls
requirements.rationale: A complete privileged account register enables effective oversight and ensures every privileged account has an identified owner and justification

requirements.requirement: Report PAM programme metrics to the IT risk committee including the number of privileged accounts, shared account exceptions, logging coverage, and findings from privileged access reviews
requirements.rationale: Management reporting ensures oversight of the PAM programme and demonstrates governance effectiveness to BNM examiners

business.summary: Malaysian financial institutions must conduct periodic access reviews and recertification to ensure that all user access remains appropriate, with privileged access reviewed at least quarterly and standard access at least semi-annually, promptly revoking any access that is no longer required.

requirements.requirement: Conduct access reviews for all privileged accounts at least quarterly, with system or data owners verifying that each privileged user still requires their elevated access for current job responsibilities
requirements.rationale: Privileged accounts carry the highest risk; quarterly reviews ensure that role changes, transfers, or project completions result in timely removal of unnecessary administrative access

requirements.requirement: Conduct access reviews for all standard user accounts at least semi-annually, with system or data owners confirming that each user's access remains appropriate for their current role
requirements.rationale: Access accumulation through role changes and lateral moves is a common risk; semi-annual reviews prevent excessive access build-up across banking systems

requirements.requirement: Ensure that access identified as no longer required during reviews is promptly revoked, with revocation completed within five business days of the review decision
requirements.rationale: Delayed revocation of unnecessary access extends the window of risk identified during the review process, undermining the purpose of the recertification exercise

technology.summary: Access reviews must be supported by IAM tooling that can extract current access entitlements, route recertification decisions to appropriate owners, track revocation actions, and provide audit evidence of the complete review cycle.

requirements.requirement: Implement an access review and recertification tool or process capable of extracting current user entitlements from all critical systems, presenting them to system owners for review, and tracking approval or revocation decisions
requirements.rationale: Manual access reviews across dozens of systems are error-prone and incomplete; tooling ensures comprehensive coverage and consistent review quality

requirements.requirement: Configure automated notifications and escalations for access reviews, ensuring review campaigns are completed within defined timelines and that overdue reviews are escalated to management
requirements.rationale: Without automated reminders and escalations, access reviews frequently stall or are completed superficially, reducing their effectiveness

requirements.requirement: Implement automated or semi-automated access revocation workflows triggered by review decisions, ensuring that revoked access is removed across all connected systems within the defined SLA
requirements.rationale: Automated revocation workflows eliminate delays between the review decision and actual access removal, closing the risk window identified during recertification

governance.summary: Access review governance requires a formal schedule and procedure, documented review outcomes with owner sign-off, revocation tracking, and management reporting on review completion rates and findings.

requirements.requirement: Maintain a formal access review schedule defining review frequency by access type (quarterly for privileged, semi-annual for standard), responsible reviewers, scope of systems, and escalation procedures for overdue reviews
requirements.rationale: A formal schedule ensures reviews occur on time and at the required frequency, and provides clear accountability for review completion

requirements.requirement: Maintain complete records of all access reviews including reviewer identity, review date, decisions made (retain or revoke), and evidence of completed revocation actions
requirements.rationale: Complete review records demonstrate compliance with BNM's recertification requirements and support audit verification of the review process

requirements.requirement: Report access review metrics to the IT risk committee including review completion rates, number of access rights revoked, overdue reviews, and trends in access accumulation
requirements.rationale: Management reporting ensures oversight of the recertification programme and enables identification of systemic access management issues
